hero.title: Aquarium Portrait Filter
hero.description: Transform ordinary portraits into immersive underwater scenes with soft caustic light and drifting fish. Ideal for eye-catching TikTok transitions, Reels, Shorts, product reveals, fashion edits, and social media ads that need a dreamy, aquatic mood.
hero.upload_instruction: Upload media to apply this template.
hero.image_prompt_before: A clean, well-lit front-facing portrait of a young adult against a neutral grey background, sharp focus, natural skin tones, minimal makeup, simple casual clothing, shot with a 50mm lens, studio lighting, no special effects.
hero.image_prompt_after: The same portrait subject with the Aquarium Portrait Filter: soft underwater light rays, gentle caustic patterns on the face and clothing, subtle blue-cyan color grading, a school of small translucent fish circling gracefully around the subject, bubbles, and a slight ethereal glow, creating a serene underwater atmosphere.

what_it_is.title: What makes a Aquarium Portrait Filter video work?
what_it_is.intro: A dreamy blend of drifting fish, caustic light rays, and soft blue-green tones turns any portrait into an immersive underwater scene. The effect relies on layered motion, gentle water optics, and a subject that feels at home in the depths.

cards.title: A Clear, Front-Facing Portrait
cards.description: Start with a well-lit, front-facing shot against a simple background. Strong contrast helps the filter separate the subject and place fish naturally around the face and shoulders.

cards.title: Slow, Floating Subject Motion
cards.description: Small, fluid head tilts or gentle swaying mimics underwater drift. The filter tracks the face, so restrained movement keeps the fish and light effects exactly where they need to be.

cards.title: Layered Light and Fish Animation
cards.description: The effect works by overlaying animated fish, shimmering water caustics, and soft bubbles. Parallax motion between layers creates depth and the feeling of being submerged.

cards.title: A Scroll-Stopping Dreamscape
cards.description: The surreal aquarium look grabs attention in fast feeds. It fits fantasy trends, adds intrigue to product teasers, and invites longer watch time on TikTok, Reels, and Shorts.

application_scenarios.title: Application Scenarios
application_scenarios.description: See how creators use the Aquarium Portrait Filter to craft immersive aquatic visuals across different content channels.

scenarios.title: Mermaid & Ocean Fantasy Cosplay
scenarios.description: Cosplayers add realistic fish schools and underwater light to transform portraits into mermaid character shoots.
scenarios.image_prompt: A cosplayer dressed as a mermaid with iridescent tail and shell top, face illuminated by soft dreamy water light rays, surrounded by a swirling school of colorful tropical fish, immersed in a deep blue aquarium atmosphere, realistic portrait photo, ethereal glow, shallow depth of field.

scenarios.title: Dreamy Instagram Aesthetic Posts
scenarios.description: Lifestyle creators use the filter to produce soft, flowing portraits that stand out in social feeds.
scenarios.image_prompt: A young woman with a calm expression, wearing a flowing white dress, submerged in crystal-clear water with gentle caustic light patterns dancing on her skin, a group of koi fish circling gracefully around her, wide-angle composition, airy and tranquil mood, editorial style.

scenarios.title: Artistic Portrait Sessions
scenarios.description: Photographers apply the filter to offer clients a unique, painterly aquatic portrait experience.
scenarios.image_prompt: A close-up portrait of a person with water droplets on their face, soft diffused underwater light creating a halo effect, small neon tetras and a gentle jellyfish drifting near the lens, blurred background suggesting deep water, ethereal and modern fine-art portrait, high detail.

faqs.question: What is the Aquarium Portrait Filter effect?
faqs.answer: The Aquarium Portrait Filter adds soft water light and fish that appear to circle around the subject, giving photos or videos an underwater portrait feel.

faqs.question: Why is Aquarium Portrait Filter useful for TikTok, Reels, or Shorts?
faqs.answer: It creates a distinctive visual that grabs attention in a fast-scrolling feed, helping short-form content stand out and increase viewer engagement.

faqs.question: Can I create the Aquarium Portrait Filter effect from one uploaded image?
faqs.answer: Yes, most tools that offer this effect let you apply it to a single photo. The filter isolates the subject and blends it into an aquarium-themed scene automatically.

faqs.question: What kind of image works best with the Aquarium Portrait Filter?
faqs.answer: Portraits or images with a clear main subject and decent lighting deliver the most natural result. Strong contrast between the subject and background helps the filter blend water and fish more seamlessly.

faqs.question: Can I use the Aquarium Portrait Filter for product, fashion, travel, or creator content?
faqs.answer: Yes, it works well across content types. It can give product shots a fresh look, add a creative layer to fashion editorials, make travel photos more playful, or help creator posts catch the eye on social media.

cta.headline: Make an aquarium portrait from any photo
cta.supporting_text: Start from a prompt, upload your own photo, or use a template.

other_names.title: Other names for Aquarium Portrait Filter
other_names.description: You might come across this effect under different names depending on what catches your eye first — the drifting fish, the soft underwater glow, or the bubble-filled backdrop. Some people search for it as an underwater portrait filter, while others call it a fish tank photo effect or an aquatic selfie filter. Whatever phrase comes to mind, LazyKiwi makes it easy to transform those ideas into polished, short-video-ready visuals that look like they were filmed inside a dreamy aquarium.
